fault_hypothesis: On line 68, the mean is computed by dividing by `window` instead of `len(tail)`. When fewer samples than `window` are available, this produces a deflated result rather than the true average of available samples.

constraints.intent: mean should be total divided by the number of available samples, not by window
after computing mean, mean * len(tail) should equal total

spec.expr: len(tail__1) == 0 or (mean__1 >= total__1 / window if len(tail__1) == window else mean__1 >= total__1 / window)

constraints.intent: when tail is shorter than window, dividing by window underestimates the mean

spec.expr: len(tail__1) > 0 and (len(tail__1) == window or len(tail__1) < window)

constraints.intent: detect case where tail is shorter than window so division denominator matters

spec.expr: __cbfl_result is None or abs(__cbfl_result - sum([_clamp_sample(v) for v in values[-window:]]) / len(values[-window:])) < 1e-9

constraints.intent: return value must equal the mean of available clamped tail samples
